title: Global Box Office 2025 Report: Key Findings
text:
Overall Performance: The global box office closed 2025 at an estimated $33.5 billion, representing nearly 12% growth compared to 2024. Excluding China, the increase was approximately 8%, marking recovery from the previous year's 11% decline.

Major Studio Winners:

Disney dominated the year, reaching $6.58 billion worldwide ($4.08B internationally). Key performers included Lilo & Stitch (first billion-dollar film of 2025), Zootopia 2 (exceeded $1 billion globally), and Avatar: Fire and Ash (crossed the $1 billion threshold).

Warner Bros. achieved nearly $4.4 billion across just 11 titles, compared to 20 films the previous year, demonstrating quality over quantity strategy.

International Market Highlights:

China generated approximately $7.4 billion, with Ne Zha 2 dominating as the year's highest-grossing film globally at $2.2+ billion—nearly 30% of China's total box office.

Japan emerged as standout performer, showing roughly 35% growth in local currency, buoyed by anime successes including Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba – Infinity Castle and Chainsaw Man.

Anime's Rise: The medium significantly expanded globally, drawing previously underserved audiences while becoming must-sees in all regions.

Notable Challenges: France experienced a 13.6% decline in admissions, while Korea struggled as local series competition intensified. Some Asian markets remained more than 50% below 2019 performance levels.

Distribution Trends: Industry executives emphasized that windowing is not a bad thing, though shortened windows have shifted consumer habits from attending theatrical events generally to seeing specific movies exclusively at home.
